### Render Interactive Canvas with Simple RadarChart Story Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Displays an interactive Canvas component showing the Simple RadarChart story with padded layout. This provides a visual example of the RadarChart component in action within the Storybook documentation. ```JavaScript ``` -------------------------------- ### Display Component Properties with ArgTypes Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Renders an interactive ArgTypes table displaying all configurable properties of the RadarChart component, sorted with required properties first. This allows developers to explore and understand the component's API directly in the documentation. ```JavaScript ``` -------------------------------- ### Display Multivariate Data with Radar Chart in Recharts Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Visualize and compare multiple variables across categories using a radar/spider chart format. Displays multivariate data for two entities (Student A and Student B) with filled areas, custom colors, and configurable opacity. Uses PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, and PolarRadiusAxis for proper polar coordinate system setup. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { RadarChart, PolarGrid, PolarAngleAxis, PolarRadiusAxis, Radar, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { subject: 'Math', A: 120, B: 110, fullMark: 150 }, { subject: 'Chinese', A: 98, B: 130, fullMark: 150 }, { subject: 'English', A: 86, B: 130, fullMark: 150 }, { subject: 'Geography', A: 99, B: 100, fullMark: 150 }, { subject: 'Physics', A: 85, B: 90, fullMark: 150 }, { subject: 'History', A: 65, B: 85, fullMark: 150 } ]; function RadarChartExample() { return ( ); } export default RadarChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Storybook Meta Configuration for RadarChart Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Configures the Storybook meta information for the RadarChart component using the imported RadarChartStories module. This establishes the component context and metadata for the documentation page. ```JavaScript ``` -------------------------------- ### Import Storybook Documentation Components and RadarChart Stories Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/RadarChart.mdx Imports Storybook addon-docs blocks (Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes) for documentation rendering and the RadarChart stories module. These imports enable interactive documentation generation and story composition in Storybook. ```JavaScript import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as RadarChartStories from './RadarChart.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Recharts Polygon Component (JSX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Polygon.mdx Demonstrates how to render the `Polygon` component in Recharts, passing its properties as JSX attributes. This component is a fundamental building block for other Recharts elements like `Radar` charts. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Create a multi-type Composed Chart with Recharts in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component demonstrates how to use Recharts' ComposedChart to display multiple chart types (Area, Bar, Line) on a single visualization. It combines Line, Area, and Bar components with XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, and Legend to illustrate complex data relationships. The chart is wrapped in a ResponsiveContainer for flexible sizing, and it takes an array of data objects as input. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ComposedChart, Line, Area, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Jan', uv: 590, pv: 800, amt: 1400, cnt: 490 }, { name: 'Feb', uv: 868, pv: 967, amt: 1506, cnt: 590 }, { name: 'Mar', uv: 1397, pv: 1098, amt: 989, cnt: 350 }, { name: 'Apr', uv: 1480, pv: 1200, amt: 1228, cnt: 480 }, { name: 'May', uv: 1520, pv: 1108, amt: 1100, cnt: 460 }, { name: 'Jun', uv: 1400, pv: 680, amt: 1700, cnt: 380 }, ]; function ComposedChartExample() { return ( ); } export default ComposedChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### ComposedChart Component Structure Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ComposedChart.mdx The ComposedChart component is a container for composed chart visualizations that can display multiple data series with different chart types simultaneously. It requires a ResponsiveContainer parent and supports multiple child components for customization. ```APIDOC ## ComposedChart Component ### Description A flexible charting component that combines multiple chart types (Area, Line, Bar) in a single visualization. Used for displaying complex data with multiple dimensions and series. ### Component Hierarchy #### Parent Component - **ResponsiveContainer** - Required wrapper component that provides responsive sizing #### Child Components (Supported) - **XAxis** - Horizontal axis component - **YAxis** - Vertical axis component - **CartesianGrid** - Grid lines for better readability - **Tooltip** - Interactive data point information display - **Legend** - Chart legend for data series identification - **Area** - Area chart data series - **Line** - Line chart data series - **Bar** - Bar chart data series - **ReferenceArea** - Highlighted area reference zone - **ReferenceLine** - Horizontal or vertical reference line - **ReferenceDot** - Reference point marker - **Brush** - Data range selector component - **Customized** - Custom shape components - **Valid SVG Elements** - Additional SVG elements for custom rendering ### Properties Refer to ArgTypes documentation for complete property listing. Note: Properties in the 'Other' and 'Internal' groups are not recommended for use. ### Basic Usage Example ```jsx import { ComposedChart, Area, Line, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Jan', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 }, { name: 'Feb', uv: 3000, pv: 1398, amt: 2210 } ]; ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Add CartesianGrid, XAxis, and YAxis to Recharts LineChart (TypeScript/JSX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/GettingStarted.mdx This code expands on the basic line chart by incorporating essential visual components like `CartesianGrid`, `XAxis`, and `YAxis`. It imports the necessary components and configures the X-axis to display data based on the 'name' key, enhancing readability and data interpretation. ```tsx import { LineChart, Line, CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis } from 'recharts'; const renderLineChart = ( ); ``` ```tsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Scatter Chart with Multi-dimensional Data in Recharts Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Create a scatter plot visualization with multiple data series, Z-axis scaling for bubble sizes, and custom cell colors. Supports multiple scatter groups with different shapes and styling options. Uses CartesianGrid, Tooltip, and Legend components for enhanced interactivity and data clarity. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ScatterChart, Scatter, XAxis, YAxis, ZAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer, Cell } from 'recharts'; const data01 = [ { x: 100, y: 200, z: 200 }, { x: 120, y: 100, z: 260 }, { x: 170, y: 300, z: 400 }, { x: 140, y: 250, z: 280 }, { x: 150, y: 400, z: 500 }, { x: 110, y: 280, z: 200 } ]; const data02 = [ { x: 300, y: 300, z: 200 }, { x: 400, y: 500, z: 260 }, { x: 200, y: 700, z: 400 }, { x: 340, y: 350, z: 280 }, { x: 560, y: 500, z: 500 } ]; const COLORS = ['#0088FE', '#00C49F', '#FFBB28', '#FF8042', '#8884d8']; function ScatterChartExample() { return ( {data01.map((entry, index) => ( ))} ); } export default ScatterChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement a Recharts AreaChart with linear gradients and multiple data series in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component showcases a Recharts AreaChart, designed to visualize cumulative data. It includes custom linear gradients for two data series ('uv' and 'pv'), a Cartesian grid, X and Y axes, and a tooltip. It depends on 'recharts' and 'react' for rendering. The data is an array of objects, each containing a 'name' and multiple value properties. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { AreaChart, Area, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: '00:00', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 }, { name: '03:00', uv: 3000, pv: 1398, amt: 2210 }, { name: '06:00', uv: 2000, pv: 9800, amt: 2290 }, { name: '09:00', uv: 2780, pv: 3908, amt: 2000 }, { name: '12:00', uv: 1890, pv: 4800, amt: 2181 }, { name: '15:00', uv: 2390, pv: 3800, amt: 2500 }, { name: '18:00', uv: 3490, pv: 4300, amt: 2100 }, { name: '21:00', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 }, ]; function AreaChartExample() { return ( ); } export default AreaChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Brush Component - Interactive Data Filtering and Zooming Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Implements an interactive brush selector to filter and zoom through large datasets in a line chart. The Brush component displays a preview of data with adjustable range handles (startIndex and endIndex). It accepts an onChange callback to track range modifications and works with ResponsiveContainer for responsive layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, Brush, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const generateData = () => { const data = []; for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) { data.push({ name: `Point ${i}`, value: Math.random() * 1000 + 100, }); } return data; }; const data = generateData(); function BrushExample() { return ( {/* Brush for data filtering */} console.log('Brush range:', range)} > ); } export default BrushExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Create a RadialBarChart with Recharts in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This snippet demonstrates how to render a RadialBarChart component using Recharts in a React application. It configures the chart with sample data, sets properties like inner and outer radii, and includes essential sub-components such as RadialBar, Legend, and Tooltip for comprehensive data representation. The chart is wrapped in a ResponsiveContainer to ensure it adapts to its parent's dimensions. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { RadialBarChart, RadialBar, Legend, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: '18-24', uv: 31.47, fill: '#8884d8' }, { name: '25-29', uv: 26.69, fill: '#83a6ed' }, { name: '30-34', uv: 15.69, fill: '#8dd1e1' }, { name: '35-39', uv: 8.22, fill: '#82ca9d' }, { name: '40-49', uv: 8.63, fill: '#a4de6c' }, { name: '50+', uv: 2.63, fill: '#d0ed57' }, { name: 'Unknown', uv: 6.67, fill: '#ffc658' }, ]; function RadialBarChartExample() { return ( ); } export default RadialBarChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Responsive Charts with Recharts ResponsiveContainer in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component illustrates the use of Recharts' ResponsiveContainer to create charts that automatically adapt to their parent container's size. It shows examples of percentage-based sizing, aspect ratio constraints, and how to use the debounce and onResize props for performance optimization and callback functionality. The ResponsiveContainer wraps LineChart instances, ensuring fluid and dynamic chart layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ResponsiveContainer, LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Page A', uv: 4000 }, { name: 'Page B', uv: 3000 }, { name: 'Page C', uv: 2000 }, { name: 'Page D', uv: 2780 }, { name: 'Page E', uv: 1890 }, ]; function ResponsiveExample() { return (
{/* Percentage-based sizing */} {/* Aspect ratio constraint */} {/* With resize callback and debounce */} console.log(`Chart resized to ${width}x${height}`)} >
); } export default ResponsiveExample; ```
